Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. I Built an Open-World Engine for the N64 [video] 136 upvotes, 14 comments
  2. ISBN Visualization 203 upvotes, 34 comments
  3. Paper Tape Is All You Need – Training a Transformer on a 1976 Minicomputer 71 upvotes, 10 comments
  4. Desperately Seeking Space Friends 28 upvotes, 2 comments
  5. Matadisco – Decentralized Data Discovery 34 upvotes, 4 comments
  6. Nashville library launches Memory Lab for digitizing home movies 172 upvotes, 42 comments
  7. Byte Interviews Chuck Peddle, Father of the MOS 6502 and Commodore PET (1982) 27 upvotes, 3 comments
  8. Velxio 2.0 – Emulate Arduino, ESP32, and Raspberry Pi 3 in the Browser 173 upvotes, 47 comments
  9. I put all 8,642 Spanish laws in Git – every reform is a commit 389 upvotes, 129 comments
  10. Improved Git Diffs with Delta, Fzf and a Little Shell Scripting 27 upvotes, 4 comments
  11. The bee that everyone wants to save 184 upvotes, 61 comments
  12. Cocoa-Way – Native macOS Wayland compositor for running Linux apps seamlessly 171 upvotes, 60 comments
  13. Anatomy of the .claude/ folder 540 upvotes, 236 comments
  14. Arm releases first in-house chip, with Meta as debut customer 54 upvotes, 17 comments
  15. Go Naming Conventions: A Practical Guide 49 upvotes, 17 comments
  16. LG's new 1Hz display is the secret behind a new laptop's battery life 283 upvotes, 141 comments
  17. CERN uses tiny AI models burned into silicon for real-time LHC data filtering 191 upvotes, 95 comments
  18. AMD's Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 Dual Edition crams 208MB of cache into a single chip 232 upvotes, 120 comments
  19. Go hard on agents, not on your filesystem 473 upvotes, 271 comments
  20. Show HN: Twitch Roulette – Find live streamers who need views the most 157 upvotes, 87 comments
  21. Make macOS consistently bad unironically 467 upvotes, 324 comments
  22. Folk are getting dangerously attached to AI that always tells them they're right 34 upvotes, 15 comments
  23. Britain today generating 90%+ of electricity from renewables 244 upvotes, 164 comments
  24. A single-file C allocator with explicit heaps and tuning knobs 37 upvotes, 19 comments
  25. Gerard of Cremona 10 upvotes, 3 comments
  26. ‘Energy independence feels practical’: Europeans building mini solar farms 330 upvotes, 300 comments
  27. No one is happy with NASA's new idea for private space stations 58 upvotes, 45 comments
  28. AI chatbots are "Yes-Men" that reinforce bad relationship decisions, study finds 85 upvotes, 75 comments
  29. Iran-linked hackers breach FBI director's personal email 340 upvotes, 454 comments
  30. Toma (YC W24) is hiring a Senior/Staff Eng to build AI automotive coworkers 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 11:30pm, 28 March 2026. Source here.
